title: Kelp DNA extractions - HMW
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"27ipk","text":"DNA extraction protocol for Durvillaea antarctica with extremely high polysaccharide levels.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 500mg of freeze dried kelp powder to 7mls of lysis buffer (4% CTAB, 2% PVP 40K, 80mM borax, 250mM EDTA, 20mM tris pH 8) and 20ul proteinase K. This solution is extremely viscous, it couldn't pipette it - instead I poured it into a 15ml tube.
Mix together and lyse overnight at 65 degrees
Cool to room temperature and add 7mls of chloroform:isoamyl alchol, mix thoroughly, centrifuge for 20 minutes at 4,000rcf
Transfer upper phase to new tube and add 0.5 volumes of 10% CTAB and 0.25 volumes of 500mM borax. Mix thoroughly.
Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 4,000rcf and transfer supernatant to new tube (easier to pour it into new tubes than to pipette it).
Cool to room temperature and add an equal volume of chloroform isoamyl alcohol. Mix thorougly.
Transfer upper phase to new tube and repeat steps 7-8.
To the upperphase, add 1 volume of 100% isopropanol. You may notice that the isopropanol isn't fully miscible in the solution, add 1ml of H2O at a time till the solution is mostly miscible.
Incubate on ice for 10 minutes, then centrifuge at 4,000rcf for 5 minutes.
Pour off supernatant and add 5mls of 70% Ethanol and mix thoroughly through inversion (use a pipette tip full of 70% ethanol to disoldge the pellet). Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 4,000rcf
Remove supernatant, spin briefly, then remove any residual ethanol.
Dry pellet (I do this using a vacuum centrifuge set to dessicate mode)
Add 500ul to 1ml of H2O to 10mM Tris to resuspend
creator: William S Pearman
